title: Question 1.1 (Butler et al.)
statement_text: Does there exist a constant $\alpha > 0$ independent of $n$, such that $HG(K_{n,n}) \geq n^\alpha$ for sufficiently large $n$?
context_text: Butler et al. posed this question in 2008 following results showing $\Omega(\log n) = HG(K_{n,n}) \leq n+1$. It asks whether the hat guessing number of the complete bipartite graph $K_{n,n}$ grows as at least a fixed positive power of $n$, despite having clique number only 2.
paper_contribution: Answers affirmatively in a generalized sense by proving $HG(K_{n,\ldots,n}) = \Omega\!\left(n^{\frac{r-1}{r}-o(1)}\right)$ for the complete $r$-partite graph with equal part sizes, implying in particular $HG(K_{n,n}) = \Omega\!\left(n^{\frac{1}{2}-o(1)}\right)$.
notes: Explicitly labelled Question 1.1 and attributed to Butler et al. [9] (2008). PDF source — exponent notation in surrounding display math may be garbled.
paper_title: The hat guessing number of graphs

title: Problem 1.4
statement_text: Do there exist functions $f_i : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{N}$, $1 \leq i \leq 3$ such that (i) if the maximum degree of $G$ is $\Delta$, then $HG(G) \leq f_1(\Delta)$; (ii) if $G$ is $d$-degenerate, then $HG(G) \leq f_2(d)$; (iii) if the minimum degree of $G$ is $\delta$, then $HG(G) \geq f_3(\delta)$, and $f_3(\delta)$ tends to infinity when $\delta$ tends to infinity.
context_text: The authors seek to understand how basic graph parameters control the hat guessing number. The existence of $f_1$ is already known via the Lovász Local Lemma ($HG(G) < e^\Delta$), but whether $f_2$ (degeneracy bound) or $f_3$ (minimum-degree lower bound) exist is unclear. Theorems 1.6 and 1.8 provide partial progress toward part (ii).
paper_title: The hat guessing number of graphs

title: Open question: exact value of $HG(K_{n,n})$
statement_text: What is the exact value of $HG(K_{n,n})$?
context_text: After establishing $\Omega\!\left(n^{\frac{1}{2}-o(1)}\right) = HG(K_{n,n}) \leq n+1$ by combining results of Gadouleau–Georgiou with Theorem 1.2, the authors note that the determination of the exact value of $HG(K_{n,n})$ is left as an interesting open question.
notes: No labelled environment; stated as an open question in running prose immediately after Theorem 1.2.
paper_title: The hat guessing number of graphs
